name: Jira to GitHub PR creator
description: Watch a Jira Cloud project for issues with a configurable label and automatically open a GitHub pull request for each new issue found.
prompt: Set up a Jira automation to create pull requests
exampleImplementation:
Trigger: cron polling (e.g. every 5 minutes)
Required secrets: Jira API token, GitHub personal access token (repo + workflow scope)

1. Collect Jira base URL, email, API token secret name, target GitHub repo, label to watch, and cron schedule from the user.
2. Poll POST /rest/api/3/search/jql on the Jira Cloud instance to find open issues carrying the configured label.
3. Deduplicate against a KV-store-backed set of already-processed issue keys so re-runs never create duplicate PRs.
4. For each new issue, start an independent OpenHands agent conversation that clones the GitHub repo, creates a branch named after the Jira key, implements or scaffolds the requested change, and opens a pull request.
5. Persist the processed issue key immediately after dispatching so the next poll skips it.
